stub: She is a Conservative MP who comes from a white working class background.
personalization: She tends to vote in accordance with her own beliefs, even when that conflicts with the position of her party.
substantive: She opposes the building of the new HS2 railway between major UK cities.
justification: In political debates, she regularly explains how her policies matter to people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background).
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
text: She is a Conservative MP who comes from a white working class background. She is from your constituency. She tends to vote in accordance with her own beliefs, even when that conflicts with the position of her party. She opposes the building of the new HS2 railway between major UK cities. In political debates, she regularly explains how her policies matter to people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background). Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
